title: kernelの要件定義について、MBPとmacminiの差分を埋めて。更に以下使用できるか検討せよ。https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins
summary_text: 1. **Critical: `/kernel` slash UX is not production-supported in Codex chat UI** - Evidence: [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/README.md#L24) explicitly says local launcher does not change native slash support, and bare `/kernel` in chat is not a supported local SLO path ([README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/README.md#L25)). - Operational risk: operators think `/kernel` is canonical, hit “Unrecognized command,” and fail over manually under pressure. - Minimum production-safe remedy: treat shell commands (`kernel`, `k`, `vote`, `v`, `fugue`) as the only supported entrypoints; ban bare chat slash commands in runbooks/on-call SOP. 2. **Critical: Host parity drift in command stack (MBP vs mac mini)** - Evidence MBP: shell routes `kernel`/`vote` to shared launcher ([.zshrc](/Users/masayuki/.zshrc#L759), [.zshrc](/Users/masayuki/.zshrc#L792)). - Evidence mac mini: shell uses ad-hoc local wrappers and inline prompt parsing ([~/.zshrc on mac mini]( /Users/masayuki_otawara/.zshrc ) lines 235-287 from live check), not the same launcher contract. - Operational risk: same command name, different semantics and failure behavior across hosts. - Minimum production-safe remedy: one repo-managed launcher path for both hosts, one shared shell snippet, no host-specific inline parser logic. 3. **High: mac mini `vote` path bypasses guard/bootstrap invariants** - Evidence: mac mini `vote` calls `_raw_codex_start_named_prompt` and invokes raw codex directly ([~/.zshrc on mac mini]( /Users/masayuki_otawara/.zshrc ) lines 235-275); no `~/bin/vote` or shared launcher binary exists on mini (live `ls` check). - Contrast: launcher contract requires guard/diversity/bootstrapped lanes ([README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/README.md#L16), [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/README.md#L20)). - Operational risk: `vote` on mini can run without production gating that MBP expects. - Minimum production-safe remedy: route mini `vote` through the same `codex-prompt-launch` + `codex-kernel-guard` path as MBP. 4. **High: Prompt source precedence is host-global first for canonical commands** - Evidence: canonical prompts prefer `~/.codex/prompts/*` before repo-local prompt files ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L26)). - Operational risk: unnoticed host-local prompt drift changes behavior in production even when repo state is identical. - Minimum production-safe remedy: pin production to versioned prompt pack or enforce hash check of global prompts before launch. 5. **High: Live smoke can report green without true live-lane verification** - Evidence A: kernel smoke exits success immediately when `CODEX_KERNEL_SYNTHETIC_SMOKE` is off (default `0`) ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L884), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L887)). - Evidence B: vote smoke prints PASS even if test script is absent because the test is conditional on file existence ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L864), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L870)). - Evidence C: cached bootstrap evidence can be reused for up to 600s ([codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L544), [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L699)). - Operational risk: false-positive “healthy” signal during real degradation. - Minimum production-safe remedy: production smoke must force synthetic+after-bootstrap mode, require vote smoke test artifact presence, and disable or tighten cached evidence for health checks. 6. **Medium: `fugue` doctrine on mac mini is still baseline-trio (`codex+claude+glm`) hard-biased** -
